title: Manuscript Artifact v2.0
description: The manuscript.v2.0 artifact contract. Produced by Worker 1 (v5.0+). Implements Doc 22 v1.0.1 normalization rules and W1 Invariants I-1 through I-10. This is a design sketch; see companion README.md for open questions.

schema_version.description: Schema version. v2.0 artifacts MUST declare "2.0". Per I-8, this value MUST match the schema-version segment of the artifact's storage key.

worker_version.description: Worker 1 semantic version (e.g., "5.0.0" or "5.0.0-a1"). SemVer 2.0-compliant pre-release / build suffixes accepted. Per I-8, this MUST match the w{worker_version} segment of the storage key.

rules_version.description: Doc 22 rule-set version (e.g., "1.0.1"). Per I-8, this MUST match the r{rules_version} segment of the storage key. Traceability back to the rule set that produced the artifact.

artifact_type.description: Artifact type discriminator. Always "manuscript" for this schema.

artifact_id.description: Unique id for this specific artifact instance.

service_id.description: Airtable Service record id this artifact was produced for.

parent_artifacts.description: Optional lineage — prior artifacts this one derives from. Typically empty for manuscript.v2.0 (the source is a DOCX, not another artifact).

blocks.description: Ordered blocks per I-3 (document order preserved).

stats.description: Optional summary stats (word count, chapter count, etc.). Informational; rules do not consume.

applied_rules.description: Per I-9: every L1b rule that fired and every L5 default applied, with provenance. Absence of an entry means the rule did not fire.

warnings.description: Per I-9: every Layer 3 warning raised during validation.

rule_faults.description: Per I-7: exceptions raised during rule execution. Empty on a clean run. Fault-safe emission means the artifact still ships even when this array is non-empty; the Service state transition is governed by the Operational Policy fault-threshold config.

manuscript_meta.description: Top-level manuscript metadata extracted by C-003 (title page) from the author-supplied opening cluster. Optional — absent when no title_page cluster was classified. Any field may be null when the classifier could not extract it confidently. Intake-vs-manuscript reconciliation (v1.1 punchlist) reads from this object on the extraction side.

title.description: Book title extracted from the title-page cluster.

id.description: Stable unique block id per I-1. Six-digit zero-padded sequence.

source.description: Extractor-specific origin pointer. Survives serialization (per CIR §Block Structure).

type.description: Structural CIR type from the extractor. Set before Classify phase.

text.description: Plain-text content. Mutually exclusive with spans[].

spans.description: Rich-formatted content. Mutually exclusive with text.

style_tags.description: Frozen CIR style-tag vocabulary (Doc 22 §CIR Block Structure). Extractors MUST NOT emit tags outside this set without a schema revision.

preformatted.description: Verbatim-content flag. Orthogonal to type; can be true on any block type. preformatted_block always carries true.

footnote_ref.description: Present only on footnote blocks. Id of the origin paragraph that anchored this footnote in source.

source_paragraph_id.description: Present on the two halves produced when an inline page break splits a source paragraph. Shared across both halves.

role.description: Semantic role assigned during Classify phase per Doc 22 §Layer 2 (v1.0.1 enum). Required on every block per I-2; never null. Terminal default applies when no classifier fires — see Doc 22 v1.0.1 Patch 2. NOTE: CIR-type → role mapping is pending in Doc 22 v1.0.2 (schema README, open question Q1).

classification_notes.description: Populated when the classifier used its unknown exit, hit terminal default, or otherwise recorded uncertainty.

chapter_number.description: Present on role=chapter_heading. Integer when extractable from heading text; null when unextractable. String for e.g. "I" / "Five" where the manuscript uses non-arabic numbering (per C-001).

chapter_title.description: Present on role=chapter_heading. Per I-4 (v1.0.1) never null and never empty. Synthesized as "Chapter <n>" for number-only headings per C-001 v1.0.1.

force_page_break.description: Per I-5, always true on role=part_divider blocks. Downstream renderers may treat as authoritative.

subtype.description: Present on role=front_matter (e.g., dedication, preface, foreword, introduction, epigraph, note_to_reader, prologue, generic) and role=back_matter (e.g., epilogue, closing_letter, acknowledgments, about_the_author, appendix, resources, notes, bibliography, references, glossary, index, generic). Per I-6, never null; "generic" is the fallback when no pattern matches.

oneOf.description: Structural blocks (page_break, horizontal_rule, scene_break, image placeholders) may omit both.

allOf.description: I-4: chapter_heading role MUST have a non-empty chapter_title.
I-5: part_divider role MUST carry force_page_break: true.
I-6: front_matter and back_matter roles MUST have a subtype.
heading CIR type MUST carry heading_level.

marks.description: Frozen CIR spans-marks vocabulary (Doc 22 §CIR Block Structure). underline and strikethrough are new in v2.0 and W2 decides whether to render or strip each.

count.description: Number of times the transformation was applied. Optional; present for count-based rules like N-004.

decision.description: For L5 defaults, the decision made (e.g., "used author title page; suppressed system-generated"). Required for L5 per I-9.

block_ids.description: Optional: the block ids this rule fired on, when per-block provenance is useful.

detail.description: Human-readable warning summary. Include the specific observation (e.g., "possible missing space: 'theweather'").

block_id.description: Optional: the block this warning is anchored to.

blocks.description: Optional: multiple block ids for warnings that span more than one block (e.g., V-001 chapter gap).

offset.description: Optional: character offset within the block's text/spans for precise anchoring.

message.description: Sanitized fault message. MUST NOT include raw manuscript content.

block_id.description: The block the rule was operating on when it faulted, when applicable.
